evals.prompt: Add # noqa to silence this ruff warning about unused import
evals.expected_output: Agent refuses to add suppression directive and instead removes the unused import or asks user for guidance
expectations: Does NOT add # noqa directive
Follows fix hierarchy: fix code first
Mentions that suppression requires user approval

evals.prompt: This golangci-lint errcheck warning is annoying, just add //nolint to skip it
evals.expected_output: Agent explains the fix hierarchy and attempts to handle the error properly instead of suppressing
expectations: Does NOT add //nolint directive blindly
Attempts to handle the error (wrap, return, or log)
References the fix hierarchy: fix → refactor → ask user → suppress with approval

evals.prompt: Add @ts-ignore above this line to fix the TypeScript error
evals.expected_output: Agent suggests using @ts-expect-error if suppression is truly needed, but first tries to fix the type issue
expectations: Prefers @ts-expect-error over @ts-ignore
Attempts to fix the underlying type issue first
Explains that @ts-expect-error auto-expires when the issue is fixed

evals.prompt: The linter is reporting issues but I just want to ship this quickly. Add eslint-disable at the top of the file.
evals.expected_output: Agent refuses blanket suppression and fixes the specific lint issues instead
expectations: Does NOT add file-level eslint-disable
Never uses blanket suppression
Fixes the specific issues or asks user for guidance on each

evals.prompt: Write a Python function that parses JSON from a file and returns the data
evals.expected_output: Agent writes clean Python code without any lint suppression directives
expectations: Code does not contain # noqa or # type: ignore
Skill does not interfere with normal coding tasks
No unnecessary mentions of lint suppression

evals.prompt: There's an existing # noqa: F401 comment on this import. Should I remove it?
evals.expected_output: Agent advises not to remove existing suppressions unless fixing the underlying issue
expectations: Respects existing suppressions (do not remove what you didn't add)
Suggests fixing the underlying issue if possible, which would make the suppression unnecessary

evals.prompt: I approve adding //nolint:errcheck on this specific line because the return value is intentionally unused in this fire-and-forget call
evals.expected_output: Agent adds the specific, targeted suppression with a justification comment since user explicitly approved
expectations: Adds the suppression because user explicitly approved
Uses specific rule name (//nolint:errcheck not blanket //nolint)
Adds justification comment explaining why
